title: Add tiered batching dashboards (latency/queue/batch size) with cardinality-safe dimensions
preview: Summary: This PR adds/updates Grafana dashboards to support the tiered batching defaults launch (fast/standard/cost-efficient). The goal is to make it easy to answer: (1) which tier is active and why, (2) whether latency SLOs are being met per tier, (3) whether we are building unsafe queue depth / wait time under lo...
text: Summary: This PR adds/updates Grafana dashboards to support the tiered batching defaults launch (fast/standard/cost-efficient). The goal is to make it easy to answer: (1) which tier is active and why, (2) whether latency SLOs are being met per tier, (3) whether we are building unsafe queue depth / wait time under load, and (4) whether batch formation is behaving as expected (size and wait budget). Dashboards added: (A) Tiered Batching — Overview: high-level latency (p50/p95/p99), request rate, error rate, and saturation widgets with tier selector; includes a ‘canary vs control’ comparison row (for staged rollout) keyed off rollout cohort label when present. (B) Tiered Batching — Latency & Streaming: end-to-end latency and time-to-first-token panels, split by tier; includes separate panels for streaming vs non-streaming routes to avoid blending distributions. (C) Tiered Batching — Queu...

title: Add metering drift Grafana dashboard + alert rules (drift, freshness, duplicates)
preview:
Context
We’re standardizing a metering data-quality view so Finance/RevOps + Eng can quickly answer: (a) are usage events in sync with the billing ledger, (b) is data arriving on time for close/invoice previews, and (c) are we seeing duplicate usage events consistent with idempotency regressions/backfills.

This PR ...
text:
Context
We’re standardizing a metering data-quality view so Finance/RevOps + Eng can quickly answer: (a) are usage events in sync with the billing ledger, (b) is data arriving on time for close/invoice previews, and (c) are we seeing duplicate usage events consistent with idempotency regressions/backfills.

This PR adds a Grafana dashboard (and initial alerting rules) for metering drift, freshness, and duplicate rates. It is intended to pair with the in-product Console sanity dashboard work (ENG-4415) and the scheduled drift job in the redwood monorepo (ENG-4421).

What’s included
- Dashboard: "Metering Sanity: Drift + Freshness" (folder: Billing/Metering)
 - Drift panels (usage_events vs billing_ledger aggregates)
 - Drift % by day (global)
 - Top-N accounts by absolute drift ($ and tokens) over last 24h / 7d
 - Drift by dimension: region, model, endpoint (chat/completions/embeddings...

title: Tiered SLO dashboards + deploy/incident annotations (Hosted vs Dedicated views)
preview: Summary: This PR adds a tiered SLO dashboard set to the observability-pack, including (1) Hosted SLO overview, (2) Dedicated SLO overview (tier-wide; no customer-level by default), and (3) a Dedicated vs Hosted comparison view. It also adds Grafana annotations for deploys and incidents so we can correlate latency sp...
text: Summary: This PR adds a tiered SLO dashboard set to the observability-pack, including (1) Hosted SLO overview, (2) Dedicated SLO overview (tier-wide; no customer-level by default), and (3) a Dedicated vs Hosted comparison view. It also adds Grafana annotations for deploys and incidents so we can correlate latency spikes / streaming disconnects / 5xx bursts with changes and incident timelines. Motivation: Part of the Q1 reliability milestone to formalize tiered SLOs + error budgets, we need canonical, tier-aware views that match the new SLI tagging at ingress and avoid metric cardinality blowups. This PR is intended to be the “default” internal dashboard surface linked from burn-rate alerts and runbooks. Out of scope: customer-facing Dedicated per-account dashboards and access control (tracked in ENG-4946 follow-ups) and any changes to SLI definitions/recording rules (those land in the...

title: Add FP8/INT8 benchmark + rollout Grafana dashboards and canary alerting
preview:
## Context
Serving Runtime is running an FP8/INT8 kernel benchmarking + staged rollout sprint. SRE/Runtime asked for a single place to monitor benchmark runs (offline) and the perf-canary rollout (online) with consistent dimensions.

This PR adds a small dashboard pack + alert rules in `observability-pack`:
- Grafan...
text:
## Context
Serving Runtime is running an FP8/INT8 kernel benchmarking + staged rollout sprint. SRE/Runtime asked for a single place to monitor benchmark runs (offline) and the perf-canary rollout (online) with consistent dimensions.

This PR adds a small dashboard pack + alert rules in `observability-pack`:
- Grafana dashboards for benchmark runs and rollout monitoring
- Prometheus recording rules to normalize TTFT/TPOT/tokens/s aggregations and reduce query cost
- Alert rules intended for perf canary gating (and incident-style triage)

## What’s included
### 1) Dashboard: **FP8/INT8 – Benchmark Runs**
Folder: `Serving Runtime / Performance`
Primary purpose: benchmark execution monitoring + sanity checks while running the matrix.
Panels:
- TTFT (ms) p50/p95 by `model`, `gpu_sku`, `kernel_mode` (baseline|fp8|int8), `region`
- TPOT (ms/token) p50/p95 (decode-heavy visibility)
- Tokens/s...

title: Add Suggestions API + funnel Grafana dashboards and beta alerting
preview: Summary: Adds a first-pass set of Grafana dashboards + alert rules for the Optimization Suggestions beta, covering (1) Suggestions API health (latency/errors/payload) and (2) the Suggestions view funnel (impression → expand → dismiss → apply-click), including breakdowns by org/model/route/region with conservative ca...
text: Summary: Adds a first-pass set of Grafana dashboards + alert rules for the Optimization Suggestions beta, covering (1) Suggestions API health (latency/errors/payload) and (2) the Suggestions view funnel (impression → expand → dismiss → apply-click), including breakdowns by org/model/route/region with conservative cardinality controls. Motivation: during dogfood we had a p95 regression + early customer reports of “empty despite traffic” and “estimates look off”; we need a single place for SRE/Platform + product to validate the pipeline (compute freshness), the API (error/latency), and the UX funnel (are people seeing/using suggestions). Scope notes: This PR only adds dashboards/alerts in observability-pack; it assumes the backend/UI already emits the metrics/events (ENG-19402) and the Suggestions API exists (ENG-19341). No changes to application code here. Dashboards added: (A) Optimiz...

title: Audit log pipeline dashboards + alerts (ingestion latency, drop rate) with runbook links
preview:
Context: As part of the audit logging standardization effort we’re tightening operational visibility for the audit event ingestion/export path. We’ve had a couple of near-misses where ingestion lag spiked without a clear on-call landing page, and early alert drafts lacked enough context to triage quickly.

This PR a...
text:
Context: As part of the audit logging standardization effort we’re tightening operational visibility for the audit event ingestion/export path. We’ve had a couple of near-misses where ingestion lag spiked without a clear on-call landing page, and early alert drafts lacked enough context to triage quickly.

This PR adds:
- A Grafana dashboard for the audit-log ingestion pipeline (end-to-end delivery latency, ingest backlog, drop rate, retry/429/5xx, DLQ growth).
- Alert rules for delivery latency and drop rate with sane defaults + per-environment routing.
- Runbook links embedded in alert annotations and dashboard panel descriptions.

Key signals / definitions:
- delivery_latency_seconds: time from event emission at producer to ack at ingestion sink (measured via audit-ingest gateway metrics + exporter timestamps).
- drop_rate: fraction of events not successfully delivered after retry ...

title: Quality gates: dashboards + alert rules (pass rate, failure spikes, latency, flake rate)
preview: Adds first-class observability for Optimize quality gate runs. This PR introduces (1) a Grafana dashboard for the full gate-run funnel (created → running → passed/failed/overridden), (2) Prometheus alert rules for gate failure spikes, flake rate regressions, and gate execution latency/SLO breaches, and (3) runbook l...
text: Adds first-class observability for Optimize quality gate runs. This PR introduces (1) a Grafana dashboard for the full gate-run funnel (created → running → passed/failed/overridden), (2) Prometheus alert rules for gate failure spikes, flake rate regressions, and gate execution latency/SLO breaches, and (3) runbook links + consistent labels so we can slice by config_type (batching/cache/quant_profile), model_family, route_id, account_tier, and region. Motivation: we’re rolling out mandatory gating ahead of applying Optimize recommendations; we need to catch (a) real quality regressions, (b) eval-harness instability/flaky suites causing false blocks, and (c) infrastructure constraints causing timeouts (notably EU-West). Design notes: alerts avoid paging on single-run failures; they trigger on burn-rate / sustained increases and include a ‘possible flake’ annotation when rerun aggregatio...

title: Add SDK retry/timeout/streaming disconnect dashboard panels + rollout alerts
preview: Context: As part of the cross-language SDK parity rollout (standard retries/backoff, timeout model, and streaming disconnect handling), SRE requested first-class dashboards and conservative alerts to monitor load impact and customer-facing failure rates during beta/stable releases. This PR adds (1) new panels to the...
text:
Context: As part of the cross-language SDK parity rollout (standard retries/backoff, timeout model, and streaming disconnect handling), SRE requested first-class dashboards and conservative alerts to monitor load impact and customer-facing failure rates during beta/stable releases. This PR adds (1) new panels to the existing "SDK Client Health" Grafana dashboard for retry rate, timeout rate, and streaming disconnect reasons, (2) companion alert rules with rollout-friendly thresholds and burn-rate style paging, and (3) a small set of recording rules to make the queries cheaper/consistent across environments.

What’s included: - Dashboard panels: Retry rate (by sdk_language, endpoint, model, status_code), Retry attempts distribution (p50/p95 of retry_count per request), Timeout rate (by sdk_language, timeout_scope=overall|connect|read), Timeout duration (histogram heatmap where availabl...

title: Add audit log lag dashboard + alerts and wire runbook links (ENG-4824)
preview: Context: Support + CS escalations (notably around audit log delivery latency) highlighted that we lacked a first-class, Support/CS-friendly view of end-to-end audit log pipeline lag and an alert that aligns to enterprise expectations. This PR adds a dedicated dashboard and alerting rules for audit log lag, and ensur...
text:
Context: Support + CS escalations (notably around audit log delivery latency) highlighted that we lacked a first-class, Support/CS-friendly view of end-to-end audit log pipeline lag and an alert that aligns to enterprise expectations. This PR adds a dedicated dashboard and alerting rules for audit log lag, and ensures panels/alerts link to a canonical runbook. It is intended to reduce time-to-triage during tickets like SUP-1846 and to support the known-issues sprint playbook work by providing an authoritative signal.

What’s included: (1) New Grafana dashboard: "Audit Logs — Delivery Lag" with panels for ingestion → transform → delivery stages, p50/p95/p99 lag, backlog depth (where available), per-region breakdown, and a quick "is this customer-impacting" section (current lag vs expected SLO). (2) New Prometheus alerts: audit-log-lag warning + critical, scoped to production namespaces...

title: Add pseudonymous token-cost summarizer and privacy-hash bridge
preview: Context/Motivation: We need per-invocation token cost visibility without retaining plaintext prompt or response tokens in observability backends. This PR introduces a privacy-first pipeline: (1) deterministic, salted token-hash bucketing at the edge; (2) a compact per-invocation cost summarizer that attributes per-b...
text: Context/Motivation: We need per-invocation token cost visibility without retaining plaintext prompt or response tokens in observability backends. This PR introduces a privacy-first pipeline: (1) deterministic, salted token-hash bucketing at the edge; (2) a compact per-invocation cost summarizer that attributes per-bucket token counts and tokenized-costs; (3) a bridge service that joins hashed token buckets with model cost profiles for rollups and dashboards. Goals: provide accurate cost attribution for SLOs and billing heuristics while ensuring PII/PHI is never logged or exported. Changes are designed to be reversible (config toggles) and auditable. Main changes: - collector: add pseudonymization middleware that applies a per-tenant salt and BLAKE3-based chunked hashing to token fragments, exposing only bucket_id and hash_prefix_size metadata (no token text). - summarizer: a streaming...

title: perf-canary: add paging (Sev2/Sev1) alert rules for sustained per-model regressions
preview: Summary: This adds paging-tier perf-canary alert rules (Sev2 + Sev1) for sustained regressions on top models. These are intentionally stricter than the warning rules in pr-19179 (higher minimum sample size, longer sustain window, and stricter anti-flap) and route to the on-call rotation channel via incident-bot. Mot...
text: Summary: This adds paging-tier perf-canary alert rules (Sev2 + Sev1) for sustained regressions on top models. These are intentionally stricter than the warning rules in pr-19179 (higher minimum sample size, longer sustain window, and stricter anti-flap) and route to the on-call rotation channel via incident-bot. Motivation: During phase-1 rollout we saw warning-level regressions that were actionable when they sustained (real tail-latency impact), but the existing rules were designed to be noisy-tolerant and non-paging. This PR implements the paging path that SRE approved in the paging policy thread and is required for rollout gates integration. Alerting model: We page only for top-model + sustained regressions where we have enough canary volume to reduce false positives, and we gate on a canary-health signal (telemetry freshness + request loop healthy) to avoid paging on broken canary...

title: Add Usage Insights freshness/quality dashboard + alerts (lag + reconciliation drift)
preview: Motivation: Usage Insights is going into beta with interactive console queries backed by hourly/day partitions and reconciliation checks against billing. We need a standard dashboard + alert bundle in observability-pack so SRE/on-call can quickly answer: (1) is data fresh, (2) are we dropping/late-ingesting events b...
text:
Motivation: Usage Insights is going into beta with interactive console queries backed by hourly/day partitions and reconciliation checks against billing. We need a standard dashboard + alert bundle in observability-pack so SRE/on-call can quickly answer: (1) is data fresh, (2) are we dropping/late-ingesting events by route/model, (3) are we drifting vs billing totals beyond tolerance, and (4) are query endpoints timing out for common ranges.

This PR adds a Grafana dashboard and alert rule templates for freshness lag, late event rate, reconciliation drift, and query p95 latency. The dashboard is designed to be imported into customer-facing/internal stacks with minimal per-cluster edits (templated variables for env/region/deployment_mode/workspace_id).

What’s included: 1) Dashboard: “Usage Insights — Data Freshness & Quality” (Grafana JSON) Panels: Aggregation lag (p50/p95) by dataset...

title: Add Shadow Traffic quality delta dashboards + scoring lag panels (cardinality-safe)
preview:
## Summary
This PR adds a first-cut set of Grafana dashboards to visualize Shadow Traffic quality deltas (control vs shadow variants) and scoring pipeline lag/health. The dashboards are designed for Hosted/Dedicated/Private and intentionally avoid high-cardinality breakdowns (no prompt_id, request_fingerprint, raw r...
text:
## Summary
This PR adds a first-cut set of Grafana dashboards to visualize Shadow Traffic quality deltas (control vs shadow variants) and scoring pipeline lag/health. The dashboards are designed for Hosted/Dedicated/Private and intentionally avoid high-cardinality breakdowns (no prompt_id, request_fingerprint, raw route_id, customer_id).

This is intended to support beta oncall readiness and help triage the two main failure classes seen during dogfood/beta:
1) quality deltas missing/stale due to scoring lag or ingestion lag
2) metrics backend pressure due to accidental high-cardinality dimensions

## What’s included
- New "Shadow Traffic / Quality Deltas" dashboard:
 - Δ quality score (shadow - control) over time with confidence bands (when emitted)
 - refusal rate delta, format adherence delta
 - sample size panels (n_control, n_shadow) + effective sample rate
 - breakdowns limited t...

title: Add incident taxonomy dashboards + alerting (type/owner breakdowns + playbook links)
preview:
## Summary
This PR adds the initial Incident Taxonomy (v1) dashboards + a small set of alert rules, per the reliability program initiative. The goal is to make taxonomy classification measurable and to enable reporting/triage by incident_type + owner_team without exploding metric label cardinality.

text:
## Summary
This PR adds the initial Incident Taxonomy (v1) dashboards + a small set of alert rules, per the reliability program initiative. The goal is to make taxonomy classification measurable and to enable reporting/triage by incident_type + owner_team without exploding metric label cardinality.

Key deliverables in this PR:
- Grafana dashboard: **Incident Taxonomy (v1)**
 - incident volume and impact proxy panels (by tier, severity)
 - breakdowns by **incident_type** and **owner_team**
 - classification compliance (% unknown/unclassified)
 - MTTA/MTTR approximations using incident event timestamps
 - drilldowns from type -> owner -> recent incidents (table)
 - playbook links based on incident_type (panel links + table column)
- Prometheus rules:
 - recording rules to normalize/aggregate incident event counters into a stable set of labels
 - alert rules for (1) unclassified rate re...

title: perf-canary: add warning-level alert rules (dampening + min sample size) routed to #eng-runtime
preview:
## Summary
Adds the initial set of warning-level perf-canary alerts (no paging) to detect per-model serving performance regressions early, with explicit guardrails to reduce noise during ramp.

This PR wires:
- Warning alerts for p95 latency regression and tokens/sec drops
- Dampening (sustained windows) + min-sampl...
text:
## Summary
Adds the initial set of warning-level perf-canary alerts (no paging) to detect per-model serving performance regressions early, with explicit guardrails to reduce noise during ramp.

This PR wires:
- Warning alerts for p95 latency regression and tokens/sec drops
- Dampening (sustained windows) + min-sample conditions
- Alertmanager routing to Slack #eng-runtime
- Runbook + dashboard links in annotations

This is intended to be enabled first in us-east with a limited model list (per ENG-4880 rollout plan). Paging rules are tracked separately (pr-19183).

## Motivation / context
During early perf-canary rollout we saw false positives driven by low volume, baseline drift, and transient telemetry delays. The warning rules in this PR aim to be:
- Low-noise: only fire when there’s enough data
- Actionable: include baseline vs observed deltas + direct links
- Safe: warning-only ch...

title: Perf baseline dashboards + alert rules for representative suite (TTFT/TPOT/throughput/variance) with manifest links
preview: Context: As part of the Q1'26 perf baseline + representative workload suite, we need a standard set of dashboards and alerting that (a) make baseline runs explorable, (b) make regressions actionable, and (c) are attributable via a first-class link from the panel to the run manifest + trace artifacts. This PR adds a ...
text: Context: As part of the Q1'26 perf baseline + representative workload suite, we need a standard set of dashboards and alerting that (a) make baseline runs explorable, (b) make regressions actionable, and (c) are attributable via a first-class link from the panel to the run manifest + trace artifacts. This PR adds a versioned dashboard bundle and alert rules aimed at baseline runs + perf-canary outputs. What’s included: 1) Grafana dashboards (folder: "Perf Baselines") with panels for TTFT/TPOT percentiles, throughput, saturation proxies, and variance/noise views. 2) Alert rules for baseline regressions (non-paging by default) and canary gating signals (paging for release-branch gating only) with routing to the right channels/oncall. 3) Standard run label/templating conventions aligned with ADR-0172 (model, model_family, workload_id, region, sku, runtime_git_sha, scheduler_profile, kern...
links: Linear ENG-4828 (perf-canary integration and regression thresholds)
ADR-0172 (benchmark metrics and run manifest format)

title: Add hot-route protection dashboards + alert rules (enterprise tier + shed reasons)
preview: Context: As part of Hot-route Capacity Protection (ENG-4926), SRE needs a consistent set of dashboards + alert rules for protected routes (/v1/chat/completions, /v1/embeddings, /v1/rerank) that make it easy to answer: (1) are enterprise routes staying within SLO under fleet pressure, (2) are we shedding/throttling t...
text: Context: As part of Hot-route Capacity Protection (ENG-4926), SRE needs a consistent set of dashboards + alert rules for protected routes (/v1/chat/completions, /v1/embeddings, /v1/rerank) that make it easy to answer: (1) are enterprise routes staying within SLO under fleet pressure, (2) are we shedding/throttling the right tier first, and (3) what is the reason breakdown (quota vs overload vs admission control) when 429s occur. This PR adds Grafana dashboard definitions (jsonnet) and Prometheus alert rules for the new metrics emitted by gateway/routing/runtime (see redwood PRs for instrumentation and 429 reason codes). Scope: dashboards (per-route/per-tier latency + error + shed), annotations for protection activation events, and paging rules focused on enterprise degradation (not expected best-effort shedding). What’s included: (A) Dashboards: 1) Hot Route Protection – Overview: req...

title: Add activation funnel dashboard panels + suggested alerts for regressions
preview: Context: This is part of the Lifecycle Growth Experiments sprint (Hosted API activation + early retention). We need a standardized, self-serve dashboard that answers: (1) where do new signups drop in the funnel, (2) which experiment cohorts/variants moved the needle, and (3) how do we catch regressions quickly after...
text:
Context: This is part of the Lifecycle Growth Experiments sprint (Hosted API activation + early retention). We need a standardized, self-serve dashboard that answers: (1) where do new signups drop in the funnel, (2) which experiment cohorts/variants moved the needle, and (3) how do we catch regressions quickly after email/nudge/retargeting launches.

What’s included in this PR: Adds a set of activation funnel panels to the existing Growth/PLG dashboard pack and wires in suggested (disabled-by-default) alert rules. The dashboard focuses on signup -> API key created -> first successful request -> first 100k tokens within 7d, plus TTFT distributions.

Dashboard updates: - New row: “Activation funnel (new signups)” with conversion panels per step (absolute counts + rates), and a time series of overall activation_rate_7d (first_request within 7d of signup). - Breakdown panels: conversion b...

title: Add rollback dashboard panels + annotations for Optimization Config apply/promote/rollback events
preview: Summary: This PR updates the "Optimization Rollout Health" Grafana dashboard in observability-pack to make safe apply + rollback operations visible and diagnosable during canaries and incident response. It adds (1) dashboard annotations for Optimization Config lifecycle events (apply, promote, rollback, gate pause/f...
text: Summary: This PR updates the "Optimization Rollout Health" Grafana dashboard in observability-pack to make safe apply + rollback operations visible and diagnosable during canaries and incident response. It adds (1) dashboard annotations for Optimization Config lifecycle events (apply, promote, rollback, gate pause/fail) and (2) panels that slice the core health signals by optimization_config_version to quickly confirm whether the rollout is causing elevated error/latency or saturation. Motivation: During the gameday drill and a recent customer escalation (SUP-18421) we spent too long correlating p95 regressions with the exact config version + rollback timestamp because charts weren’t consistently annotated and most panels lacked config_version breakdown. This is a prerequisite for GA readiness (PM-904) and supports the auditability story for enterprise (SUP-18544 follow-up: 'prove rol...

title: perf-canary dashboards: add per-model p95 latency + tokens/sec panels (with breakdowns + runbook links)
preview:
## Summary
Adds the initial Grafana dashboard panels for the production performance canary. The goal is fast triage for per-model regressions (p95 latency and tokens/sec) with enough breakdowns to distinguish kernel/runtime execution regressions from scheduler/queueing and infra issues.

This PR is dashboards-only (...
text:
## Summary
Adds the initial Grafana dashboard panels for the production performance canary. The goal is fast triage for per-model regressions (p95 latency and tokens/sec) with enough breakdowns to distinguish kernel/runtime execution regressions from scheduler/queueing and infra issues.

This PR is dashboards-only (no alert rules). Alerts are tracked separately so we can iterate on panel queries/labels before we encode anything into paging logic.

## What’s included
- New dashboard: **Perf Canary / Per-model Performance (Prod)**
 - Topline panels per selected model:
 - End-to-end latency p50/p95/p99 (histogram quantiles)
 - Runtime-only latency p50/p95/p99 (when available)
 - Tokens/sec (overall) + tokens/sec (decode-only) where emitted
 - Breakdown panels:
 - Prefill latency vs decode latency (stacked / side-by-side)
 - Queueing time (scheduler) and queueing fraction of end-to-end
 -...

title: Reduce cardinality for incident_type metric label (allowlist + sampling + unknown buckets)
preview: Context: PR #1459 introduced incident taxonomy dashboards and alert rules. During rollout review, Observability flagged that naively labeling high-volume request/edge metrics with incident_type can explode series cardinality (especially when incident-bot classification is missing/late, or when teams attempt to encod...
text: Context: PR #1459 introduced incident taxonomy dashboards and alert rules. During rollout review, Observability flagged that naively labeling high-volume request/edge metrics with incident_type can explode series cardinality (especially when incident-bot classification is missing/late, or when teams attempt to encode subtypes/notes). This follow-up tightens the metric labeling contract and adjusts recording rules to keep the dashboards useful without putting Prometheus/Mimir under stress. What changed: 1) Added an explicit allowlist for incident_type values used in metrics (top-level types only, no freeform). 2) Introduced two stable buckets for missing/invalid classification: incident_type="unknown" (not set) and incident_type="unclassified" (set but not in allowlist / failed validation). 3) Moved incident_type labeling off the highest-cardinality per-route/per-model series and onto ...

title: Add dashboard panels for hardware profile selection + tail latency regressions
preview: Context: As we roll out the hardware tuning profile pack (H100/L40S first; B200 gated), oncall and perf owners need to answer two questions quickly: (1) which profile was actually selected (and why), and (2) did enabling the profile cause a tail-latency regression in a specific pool/region/model/workload slice. This...
text:
Context: As we roll out the hardware tuning profile pack (H100/L40S first; B200 gated), oncall and perf owners need to answer two questions quickly: (1) which profile was actually selected (and why), and (2) did enabling the profile cause a tail-latency regression in a specific pool/region/model/workload slice. This PR updates our Grafana dashboards in observability-pack to add segmentation by `hardware_profile` (selected profile name + version) and `hardware_profile_source` (fleet default vs per-pool vs per-deployment override), and adds focused panels for tail behavior (p95/p99) with rollout-friendly breakdowns.

What’s included (high level): Adds a new dashboard row “Hardware profile selection” to the runtime/inference overview dashboard, showing selected profile distribution, profile source distribution, and a table of “top offenders” (pool/region/model) where p95/p99 is above thr...

title: Anti-flap logic (windows + hysteresis) for status-component health signals
preview: Motivation: Our current component health rollups (used to recommend public status changes and to populate status-page dashboards) are too twitchy. Short-lived spikes (e.g., 2–3 minutes of elevated 5xx or a brief p95 regression) can flip a component between Operational ↔ Degraded multiple times, which creates noisy o...
text: Motivation: Our current component health rollups (used to recommend public status changes and to populate status-page dashboards) are too twitchy. Short-lived spikes (e.g., 2–3 minutes of elevated 5xx or a brief p95 regression) can flip a component between Operational ↔ Degraded multiple times, which creates noisy on-call workflows and undermines customer trust in the status page. This PR implements anti-flap windows and hysteresis in the status-signal evaluator so we only transition component state when a condition persists long enough, and we require a healthier condition to recover than the one that caused the degradation. Summary of changes: Adds a small state machine around each status signal (errors + latency) with configurable (1) trigger window (min duration / min samples) and (2) recovery window (min duration / min samples) plus (3) hysteresis thresholds (degrade_threshold vs...

title: Link SLO burn alerts to status page update runbook + component taxonomy
preview: Context: As part of the status page transparency upgrades, on-call needs a fast, consistent path from a page to (a) the correct status-page component taxonomy and (b) the exact runbook steps for posting/updating component statuses. Today, most of our burn-rate alerts include a generic runbook link (or no runbook at ...
text: Context: As part of the status page transparency upgrades, on-call needs a fast, consistent path from a page to (a) the correct status-page component taxonomy and (b) the exact runbook steps for posting/updating component statuses. Today, most of our burn-rate alerts include a generic runbook link (or no runbook at all), and nothing points to the canonical taxonomy/mapping guidance. This PR updates alert annotations across component-level SLO burn alerts in this repo to include: (1) a status page update runbook link, and (2) a status page component taxonomy link. It also standardizes annotation keys so incident-bot and human responders can reliably surface the right links in Slack. Scope: alert rule annotations + lint/test updates only; no threshold or routing changes. Notes: we intentionally keep the links to high-level canonical docs rather than incident-specific pages, and we avoid...

title: Add overload shedding dashboards + initial alert rules (breaker opens, shed rate)
preview: Context: As part of the Hosted circuit breakers + load shedding reliability program, we need first-class visibility into (a) when we are shedding traffic vs failing implicitly, and (b) when breakers are opening/half-opening/closing across regions/models/tiers. This PR adds Grafana dashboards and initial Prometheus a...
text: Context: As part of the Hosted circuit breakers + load shedding reliability program, we need first-class visibility into (a) when we are shedding traffic vs failing implicitly, and (b) when breakers are opening/half-opening/closing across regions/models/tiers. This PR adds Grafana dashboards and initial Prometheus alert rules for overload protection signals. Scope: (1) Dashboards: Add "Hosted API • Overload Protection" dashboard group with panels for shed rate, breaker open rate, admission decisions, runtime saturation proxies (queue depth / token saturation / memory pressure proxy), and top shed reasons. Includes recommended drilldowns by region and route family, and a safe set of labels (bounded cardinality). (2) Alerts: Add first-pass paging + ticket alerts for (i) sustained breaker-open rate, (ii) elevated shed rate for paid tiers, (iii) Retry-After header missing on overload resp...

title: Add component-level SLO dashboards + standard links for status page components
preview: Context: As part of the Status Page Transparency Upgrades initiative, on-call needs a consistent, component-level view of latency + error impact that matches the public status component taxonomy (component_id x region x capability). Today dashboards are a mix of service names (gateway/scheduler/streamer), inconsiste...
text: Context: As part of the Status Page Transparency Upgrades initiative, on-call needs a consistent, component-level view of latency + error impact that matches the public status component taxonomy (component_id x region x capability). Today dashboards are a mix of service names (gateway/scheduler/streamer), inconsistent region labels, and ad-hoc links in runbooks. This PR adds a standardized dashboard set per status component and a link registry so alerts/runbooks/status tooling can point to the same canonical URLs. What’s in this PR: (1) New Grafana dashboards for each public status component, split into latency + errors with consistent panels and templating. (2) Standard naming conventions for dashboards, folders, variables, and panel titles (to reduce “is this the right chart?” time during Sev1). (3) A generated link map (component_id/region/capability -> dashboard URL) committed und...

title: Add JSON schema + CI validation for status component registry
preview: This PR introduces a JSON Schema for the public status-page component registry and adds validation in CI so registry changes fail fast when they are malformed or violate taxonomy rules. Motivation: we’re using the registry as a source-of-truth for component ids/display names/ownership and rollout flags as part of th...
text: This PR introduces a JSON Schema for the public status-page component registry and adds validation in CI so registry changes fail fast when they are malformed or violate taxonomy rules. Motivation: we’re using the registry as a source-of-truth for component ids/display names/ownership and rollout flags as part of the Status page transparency upgrades effort; recent incidents and hotfixes showed that small typos (ids, missing fields, inconsistent region/capability labels) can cause misclassification and customer-facing status inaccuracies. What’s included: (1) New schema file `statuspage/component-registry.schema.json` that defines required fields and constraints for each component entry: `component_id` (kebab-case, stable, unique), `display_name` (human readable), `product_area` (hosted_api|streaming|embeddings|reranking|console|dedicated_control_plane), `regions` (allowed set), `capa...

title: Add KMS latency/error dashboards + alert rules (per env/region)
preview: Context: As part of the KMS integration hardening initiative we needed first-class dependency visibility for KMS across Hosted/Dedicated/Private paths. This PR adds a KMS Dependency Health dashboard and alert rules that align to the canonical error taxonomy (AccessDenied/NotFound/Throttling/Timeout/InvalidCiphertext...
text: Context: As part of the KMS integration hardening initiative we needed first-class dependency visibility for KMS across Hosted/Dedicated/Private paths. This PR adds a KMS Dependency Health dashboard and alert rules that align to the canonical error taxonomy (AccessDenied/NotFound/Throttling/Timeout/InvalidCiphertext/Unknown) and our metric namespacing conventions. Scope: (1) Dashboard: Overview + per env/region panels for request volume, latency (p50/p95/p99), error rate by category, throttling signals, and circuit breaker state; (2) Alerts: page-worthy vs ticket-worthy thresholds, with environment-aware routing (prod pages, staging tickets) and burn-rate style windows where appropriate; (3) Operator ergonomics: runbook links + log/trace pivot hints (trace_id, kms_key_id, provider, region, service); (4) Documentation: short README explaining how to use/tune the alerts and what to chec...

title: Dashboards: correlate model version change rate with rollout phases and incident/regression signals
preview: Context: As part of the model metadata schema + changelog generator rollout, we now emit structured model change events (promotions, rollbacks, metadata edits) and rollout phase transitions. Support and oncall repeatedly asked for a fast way to answer: “did this latency/regression coincide with a model version promo...
text: Context: As part of the model metadata schema + changelog generator rollout, we now emit structured model change events (promotions, rollbacks, metadata edits) and rollout phase transitions. Support and oncall repeatedly asked for a fast way to answer: “did this latency/regression coincide with a model version promotion or rollout phase change?” This PR adds Grafana panels + recording rules to correlate: (1) model version change rate, (2) rollout phase transitions (canary/A-B/GA/rollback), and (3) incident/regression signals (5xx, timeouts, p95/p99 latency, eval-guardrail failures when present). What’s included: - New dashboard: “Model Rollout Correlation” with panels for change rate over time, rollout phase annotations, and ‘before/after’ latency + error overlays. - Update existing “LLM Serving Overview” dashboard with a compact panel showing model version change rate by deployment_m...

title: Add Private minimum pack Prometheus alert rules: latency + availability (with SLO burn recording rules)
preview: Context: This PR introduces the baseline alerting + recording rules for the Redwood Private observability minimum pack, focused on the highest-impact failure modes for inference availability and latency regressions. These are intended to be safe-by-default in customer clusters (bounded cardinality, conservative pagi...
text:
Context: This PR introduces the baseline alerting + recording rules for the Redwood Private observability minimum pack, focused on the highest-impact failure modes for inference availability and latency regressions. These are intended to be safe-by-default in customer clusters (bounded cardinality, conservative paging) and to map cleanly to the SLO guidance we’ve been using in staging.

What’s included (v1):
- Recording rules (namespace: redwood_private:*) used by dashboards + alerts so queries are consistent and cheaper.
- Latency SLO burn alerts (multi-window, multi-burn) for the API gateway and runtime request path.
- Availability alerts (5xx rate + blackbox reachability) for API ingress and control plane critical services.
- Alert annotations include runbook + first steps links and a canonical dashboard deep link placeholder.
- promtool tests for rule syntax + basic evaluation fix...
links: Linear ENG-19147: Private alerts - latency and availability

Support ticket SUP-49238: Private alerts too noisy after upgrade (tuning guidance input)

text: Summary: This PR adds the first versioned Grafana dashboard bundle for the Private Observability Minimum Pack. The dashboards are designed to be shipped as immutable artifacts (checked into this repo) and later installed via `redwood-helm-charts`/`redwood-private-installer` (follow-ups). Scope: 1) Runtime overview dashboard focused on golden signals + inference-specific panels (token throughput, batching, KV cache, GPU utilization). 2) Control plane health dashboard (gateway/auth/policy/config services) with standard service golden signals and dependency panels (DB/redis if present). 3) Infra dashboard for customer cluster basics required to triage most common Private incidents (node pressure, PVC pressure, pod restart loops, scrape status). Design notes / constraints: - Private environments require bounded label cardinality; dashboards avoid templating on high-cardinality labels like...

preview:
Title: Add alert rules + routing for Dedicated max-bound saturation and sustained queue buildup

Context
Dedicated autoscaling bounds are rolling out to pilot customers. During pilot we’ve seen two recurring failure/impact modes that are easy to miss without dedicated alerting:
1) Deployment hits max bound for an ex...
text:
Title: Add alert rules + routing for Dedicated max-bound saturation and sustained queue buildup

Context
Dedicated autoscaling bounds are rolling out to pilot customers. During pilot we’ve seen two recurring failure/impact modes that are easy to miss without dedicated alerting:
1) Deployment hits max bound for an extended period while demand continues increasing (customers see queueing / p95 latency regressions; support tickets like SUP-28411).
2) Sustained queue buildup (even if not max-bound) indicating a scaling issue, forecast signal outage, or model-level throughput regression.

This PR adds:
- Prometheus alert rules for max-bound saturation and sustained queue buildup.
- Alertmanager routing to Dedicated on-call (page) vs #eng-infra (ticket/notify) depending on severity and duration.
- Rule annotations/runbook links aligned with the Dedicated autoscaler troubleshooting runbook.
...

title: Private minimum pack: add GPU saturation + OOM/resource pressure alerts for inference pods
preview: Context: Private minimum pack needs a minimal, supported set of alerts that catch the highest-impact inference failure modes (GPU saturation, GPU memory pressure, OOM kills, restart loops) without overloading customer on-calls. This PR adds GPU/resource pressure alert rules scoped to Redwood inference pods and wires...
text:
Context: Private minimum pack needs a minimal, supported set of alerts that catch the highest-impact inference failure modes (GPU saturation, GPU memory pressure, OOM kills, restart loops) without overloading customer on-calls. This PR adds GPU/resource pressure alert rules scoped to Redwood inference pods and wires them into the existing private rules bundle. It includes tuning notes from the alert-noise review and enforces label/cardinality constraints for customer Prometheus.

What’s included: (1) GPU saturation alerts (warning/ticket): sustained high GPU utilization on inference pods aggregated by cluster/namespace/workload; (2) GPU memory pressure (warning): high framebuffer utilization (FB used/total) using DCGM metrics when available; (3) OOM kill alerts (critical/page): repeated OOMKilled terminations for redwood-runtime containers over a short window; (4) restart loop alerts ...

Dashboard set covers (1) precheck failure rates + breakdown by ...
text: Summary: Adds a Grafana dashboard pack for Redwood Private upgrade health, focused on the new installer-driven upgrade workflow (prechecks -> backup -> control plane rollout -> data plane rollout -> migrations -> postchecks, with rollback entry points). Dashboard set covers (1) precheck failure rates + breakdown by check_id/severity, (2) DB migration durations and timeouts/lock waits, (3) API/control-plane error rates during upgrade windows, and (4) rollback frequency + outcomes. Motivation: SRE and Release Eng asked for a single place to validate upgrade readiness and spot regressions during beta rollouts; today we rely on scattered logs and ad-hoc queries. This PR makes upgrades observable with consistent panels and variables that work in Hosted VPC Private and on-prem (including air-gapped) where only Prometheus + Loki are available. What’s included: - New dashboard: "Private Upgra...

This PR adds Grafana dashboards and Prometheus alert rules for the exporter, intended to be deployed to...
text: Context: As part of the SIEM-ready audit export beta, we need first-class operational visibility for the audit-log-exporter service (lag, retries, adapter failures) and alerting aligned to the SLOs defined by SRE. This PR adds Grafana dashboards and Prometheus alert rules for the exporter, intended to be deployed to hosted + dedicated environments for the beta cohort. Links: Linear ENG-5168 (exporter observability), Confluence SLO doc: "Audit exporter SLOs and alerting" (owner: Sean/Rafael). Scope: (1) Grafana dashboard: Overview (lag, throughput, success rate), (2) Grafana dashboard: Destination adapters (Splunk HEC / Datadog / S3), (3) Alert rules: export lag burn, sustained adapter errors, DLQ growth, auto-paused exports, (4) Recording rules for rate-based panels to reduce query cost. Notes on semantics: Export lag is computed from exporter checkpoint watermark vs event time; alert...

title: Add onboarding KPIs dashboard (TTFS/TTFP + checklist funnel) and recommended alerts
preview:
## Summary
Adds a packaged Grafana dashboard + alert rules for the Guided Onboarding / First Production Launch initiative.

This is intended to be imported via the standard `observability-pack` rollout (same mechanism as the latency/cost packs), and targets the onboarding telemetry datasets produced by the gateway/c...
text:
## Summary
Adds a packaged Grafana dashboard + alert rules for the Guided Onboarding / First Production Launch initiative.

This is intended to be imported via the standard `observability-pack` rollout (same mechanism as the latency/cost packs), and targets the onboarding telemetry datasets produced by the gateway/control plane eventing work + derived milestone tables.

## What’s included
Dashboard: **Onboarding KPIs (Hosted API)**
- TTFS (signup → first successful 2xx request) trend
 - Median / p90 / p95 by signup cohort
 - Breakdown by `sdk_language` (via user-agent tags), `region`, and `plan_tier`
- TTFP (signup → first production traffic) trend
 - Median / p90 by signup cohort
 - Includes optional filter for "prod_traffic" classifier (see notes below)
- Step completion funnel
 - % of tenants completing each checklist step (ordered)
 - Step-to-next-step conversion + time-in-step
 -...

title: Alert tuning: GPU saturation + queue depth (reduce noise, keep early warning)
preview: Context: Over the last ~6 weeks we’ve had repeated pages for brief GPU util spikes and transient scheduler queue growth (especially during deploy waves / regional rebalancing). These pages are noisy, but we still want early warning when we’re genuinely saturating and heading toward latency/5xx SLO burn. This PR tune...
text:
Context: Over the last ~6 weeks we’ve had repeated pages for brief GPU util spikes and transient scheduler queue growth (especially during deploy waves / regional rebalancing). These pages are noisy, but we still want early warning when we’re genuinely saturating and heading toward latency/5xx SLO burn. This PR tunes the GPU saturation + queue depth alert rules used by Hosted and Dedicated pools, aligning them with the standardized saturation signals that incident-bot v2 surfaces.

What’s changing (high level): (1) GPU util paging now requires persistence and corroboration (queue depth or admission/backpressure) instead of firing on short 1–2 minute spikes. (2) Added a warning tier for early detection (route/regional) and kept a stricter paging tier for sustained saturation. (3) Queue depth alerting is now based on a smoothed p95 + “time-above-threshold” gate and avoids firing during ...

title: Add rollout cohort panels (latency + errors) and alert templates
preview: Context: As part of Hosted canary rollout GA work, we need a standardized dashboard/alert baseline that makes it easy to compare baseline vs canary cohorts (latency + errors) without every team re-deriving PromQL and without blowing up cardinality. This PR adds a small set of reusable panels + a “rollout overview” d...
text: Context: As part of Hosted canary rollout GA work, we need a standardized dashboard/alert baseline that makes it easy to compare baseline vs canary cohorts (latency + errors) without every team re-deriving PromQL and without blowing up cardinality. This PR adds a small set of reusable panels + a “rollout overview” dashboard to the observability pack, plus alert templates and recommended default thresholds for auto-stop/rollback wiring. What’s included: (1) New dashboard: “Hosted Rollouts / Cohort Comparison (Latency + Errors)” with panels for request rate, error rate, timeout rate, p50/p95 latency, and a baseline-vs-canary delta view. Panels are parameterized around {route, region, rollout_id} and assume cohort in {baseline, canary}. (2) Library panels (Jsonnet) that can be embedded into other service dashboards: p95 latency by cohort, 5xx rate by cohort, timeouts by cohort, and “cana...

title: Add Grafana dashboard: Route Experiment Guardrails (latency/error/cost + attribution drop)
preview: ## Summary This PR adds a standard Grafana dashboard for Route-level Experiments guardrails and data quality signals. The dashboard is intended to be deployed alongside the route experiments beta/GA so on-call and customers (where applicable) can quickly answer: (1) Is any variant regressing on latency, error rate, ...
text:
## Summary This PR adds a standard Grafana dashboard for Route-level Experiments guardrails and data quality signals. The dashboard is intended to be deployed alongside the route experiments beta/GA so on-call and customers (where applicable) can quickly answer: (1) Is any variant regressing on latency, error rate, or cost? (2) Are we missing experiment attribution (drop rate) such that results are untrustworthy? (3) Is traffic split behaving as expected (e.g., variant getting ~0% traffic)?

This dashboard complements the control-plane/Console views by providing a fast, infrastructure-centric view over raw telemetry, and is designed to work with the canonical attribution dimensions from ADR-048 (route_id, experiment_id, variant_id, region, model_id) while limiting cardinality blast-radius.

## What’s included ### Dashboard: "Route Experiments / Guardrails" Panels (high-level): - Overv...

title: Add ingestion-side cardinality guards for cache telemetry
preview:
## Summary
This PR implements ingestion-side cardinality guards for the new prefix/KV cache telemetry emitted by the runtime and tagged by the gateway. The goal is to prevent accidental series explosions (per-tenant/per-route/per-request-shape label combinations) while preserving the ability to break down cache sign...
text:
## Summary
This PR implements ingestion-side cardinality guards for the new prefix/KV cache telemetry emitted by the runtime and tagged by the gateway. The goal is to prevent accidental series explosions (per-tenant/per-route/per-request-shape label combinations) while preserving the ability to break down cache signals by the dimensions we actually operate on (model, route, deployment mode, region, and prompt-length/token bins).

This is the ingestion counterpart to runtime tagging + allowlists (see redwood/pr-18426) and ensures that even if a client or misconfigured gateway emits unexpected label keys/values, we either (a) rewrite to a safe aggregated bucket, or (b) drop with explicit counters so we can diagnose.

## Context / Motivation
During early canary of cache telemetry dashboards we saw a few failure modes:
- `tenant_id` and raw `route` occasionally showing up due to legacy ga...

title: Add standardized incident-context dashboards (deploy timeline, saturation overview, error budget burn)
preview: This PR adds a standardized set of dashboards intended to be linked directly from Incident Bot v2 context cards. The goal is to make the bot output actionable in the first 5 minutes by providing consistent deep links (per service/region/route/time window) for deploy correlation, saturation triage, and SLO/error-budg...
text: This PR adds a standardized set of dashboards intended to be linked directly from Incident Bot v2 context cards. The goal is to make the bot output actionable in the first 5 minutes by providing consistent deep links (per service/region/route/time window) for deploy correlation, saturation triage, and SLO/error-budget burn. The dashboards are designed to be safe/default-bounded (limited time range defaults, low-cardinality variables, and guardrails around tenant labels) and to work across Hosted + Dedicated pools where the standard metrics exist. Included dashboards: (1) Deploy Timeline (service/region) — deploy events overlay (canary vs full rollout), build SHA, and error/latency overlays; (2) Saturation Overview (pool/region) — GPU util, queue depth, KV cache pressure, scheduler backpressure/admission, and request concurrency; (3) Error Budget Burn (service/route/region) — 1h/6h bur...

title: Alerts: experiment config propagation lag + attribution anomalies (route experiments)
preview: Context: Route-level experiments rely on control-plane config propagation into the routing/policy layer. During beta we observed occasional propagation lag and a few classes of attribution anomalies (missing experiment_id/variant_id tags, variant receiving ~0 traffic due to config/eligibility issues, and split skew ...
text: Context: Route-level experiments rely on control-plane config propagation into the routing/policy layer. During beta we observed occasional propagation lag and a few classes of attribution anomalies (missing experiment_id/variant_id tags, variant receiving ~0 traffic due to config/eligibility issues, and split skew early in rollout). This PR adds Prometheus/Alertmanager rules and annotations for the standard route experiments dashboards, with SRE-reviewed runbook links and recommended severities. Scope: (1) propagation lag/staleness alerts tied to the new propagation lag metric emitted by the redwood monorepo, (2) attribution drop-rate and missing-dimension alerts for experiment metrics/traces aggregation, (3) “variant 0 traffic” detection to catch misconfigured allocations or eligibility rules. Notes: rules are written to avoid cardinality blow-ups (no per-route fanout by default); h...

title: Add recommended alert rules + SLO examples for Quickstart Template apps
preview: Context: As part of the Quickstart Templates launch, we want to ship a recommended alerting baseline that users can enable without getting paged to death. This PR adds an opt-in alert rule group for template apps (RAG/agents) plus example SLOs and burn-rate alerts. The rules are designed around the metrics emitted b...
text: Context: As part of the Quickstart Templates launch, we want to ship a recommended alerting baseline that users can enable without getting paged to death. This PR adds an opt-in alert rule group for template apps (RAG/agents) plus example SLOs and burn-rate alerts. The rules are designed around the metrics emitted by the templates + OTel collector configs from PR-156/158, and they intentionally avoid high-cardinality dimensions (no user_id, prompt hash, raw path, or tool args). What’s included: (1) SLO examples: availability + latency SLO recording rules with suggested targets and notes on how to adapt per workload; (2) Alert rules: multi-window burn-rate (fast/slow) alerts for the SLOs, plus a small set of “hard failures” (collector down, exporter errors) and “symptom alerts” (tool failure spikes, streaming disconnect spikes, retrieval empty-result spikes) with conservative defaults;...

title: Add log/trace correlation deep links for incident context
preview: Summary: Adds standardized log/trace correlation shortcuts (deep links) that incident-bot v2 and Console can embed for fast drill-down during incidents. This PR introduces a small, versioned link-template bundle in observability-pack plus validation tests to ensure URLs remain stable across releases. Motivation: In ...
text: Summary: Adds standardized log/trace correlation shortcuts (deep links) that incident-bot v2 and Console can embed for fast drill-down during incidents. This PR introduces a small, versioned link-template bundle in observability-pack plus validation tests to ensure URLs remain stable across releases. Motivation: In beta incident-bot v2 threads we repeatedly saw responders copy/paste request IDs and hunt manually across systems. We already link to dashboards (burn rate, deploy timeline, saturation), but we were missing the 1-click “take me to logs for this trace/request in the right time window” experience. This closes that gap while keeping URLs tenant-safe. Details: (1) Adds link templates for Traces and Logs with consistent parameter names and time windows, with support for service/route/region, optional trace_id, and optional request_id. (2) Adds a tiny resolver helper (used by bot...

title: Template dashboards v1: RAG + Agents + recommended alert rules (naming + publish steps)
preview: Context: DevEx Quickstart Templates (RAG + agents) are standardizing OTel instrumentation and a small set of Redwood-specific metrics/attributes. This PR ships the initial dashboard + alerts pack in `observability-pack` so template apps have a first-run observability experience and internal Support/SRE can quickly t...
text: Context: DevEx Quickstart Templates (RAG + agents) are standardizing OTel instrumentation and a small set of Redwood-specific metrics/attributes. This PR ships the initial dashboard + alerts pack in `observability-pack` so template apps have a first-run observability experience and internal Support/SRE can quickly triage common failures (401/429/timeouts/tool errors/retrieval empty). Scope: (1) dashboards (Grafana JSON) and (2) alert rules (Prometheus-style) + (3) documentation for metric naming conventions, required labels, and publish steps for release tagging. Dashboards included: (a) `Templates / RAG + Agents Overview` (golden signals + error taxonomy rollup) (b) `Templates / RAG Latency Breakdown` (gateway + model + retrieval + tool time; p50/p95; streaming disconnect rate) (c) `Templates / Retrieval Health` (retrieval hit rate, empty result rate, top-k distribution, chunking def...

title: Add TP stall dashboard panels + alert rules (heartbeat gaps, stall reasons, timeout correlation)
preview:
Context
We’ve had repeated Dedicated + high-throughput Hosted incidents where TP replicas appear healthy at the process level but are effectively wedged: requests hang, streaming timeouts rise, and autoscaling thrashes. Runtime-side instrumentation is landing in parallel (ENG-4823 / runtime PRs), but we need a first...
text:
Context
We’ve had repeated Dedicated + high-throughput Hosted incidents where TP replicas appear healthy at the process level but are effectively wedged: requests hang, streaming timeouts rise, and autoscaling thrashes. Runtime-side instrumentation is landing in parallel (ENG-4823 / runtime PRs), but we need a first-class dashboard + alerting layer to detect silent stalls early and make triage actionable.

This PR adds:
- Grafana dashboard panels for TP heartbeat gaps (per rank + aggregated), stall reason counts, and request timeout correlation by model/cluster/replica.
- Prometheus alert rules with sane defaults + paging policy alignment (page only on sustained stall/timeout correlation, ticket otherwise).
- Links from alerts to the on-call runbook and to the most relevant filters (cluster/account/model).

What’s included (panels)
1) TP Heartbeat
- Panel: “TP heartbeat age (p50/p95/p...

title: Add retention verification + compliance pack generation dashboards/alerts
preview: Summary: This PR adds the first version of the retention/compliance dashboards and alerting rules needed for the “Retention controls + compliance reporting pack” program. It introduces (1) Retention Enforcement Verification dashboards (TTL lag, purge throughput/failures, backlog) and (2) Compliance Report Pack Gener...
text:
Summary: This PR adds the first version of the retention/compliance dashboards and alerting rules needed for the “Retention controls + compliance reporting pack” program. It introduces (1) Retention Enforcement Verification dashboards (TTL lag, purge throughput/failures, backlog) and (2) Compliance Report Pack Generation SLIs (success rate + latency) with corresponding alerts. It also updates the repo README to document metric prerequisites and rollout guidance.

Context / why: We’ve had multiple incidents/tickets where enforcement was “working” but not observable at the right level (e.g., policy applied but TTL lag growing in a single region; purge jobs silently throttling after maintenance). Security/GRC also needs a customer-ready way to evidence enforcement (at least internally) and to demonstrate the compliance pack generator’s reliability as we start using it for enterprise secu...

title: Add Dedicated staged rollout dashboards + alert templates (stage p95/p99, error rate, GPU saturation, promotion annotations)
preview: Summary: This PR adds a first cut of dashboard + alert templates for the Dedicated staged rollout system. The dashboards are stage-aware (stage_id / stage_name) and are designed to support the rollout gate model in the policy engine (latency, errors, saturation/headroom, GPU health proxies). It also adds annotations...
text:
Summary: This PR adds a first cut of dashboard + alert templates for the Dedicated staged rollout system. The dashboards are stage-aware (stage_id / stage_name) and are designed to support the rollout gate model in the policy engine (latency, errors, saturation/headroom, GPU health proxies). It also adds annotations for stage promotion/abort/hold events so operators can correlate metric shifts with rollout actions.

Context / dependencies: This is intended to ship alongside the redwood monorepo changes that (a) emit stage tags in metrics/traces and (b) provide audit events for promote/abort/hold. Dashboards assume the following labels are present on the relevant metrics: dedicated_pool_id, rollout_plan_id, stage_id, stage_name, region. For cardinality safety, the dashboards intentionally avoid any per-request labels and only group-by the allowlisted stage identifiers.

What’s included...

title: Dedicated throughput SLO: add dashboard panels for floor tracking, saturation, and drilldowns
preview: Context: We’re rolling out a formal Dedicated throughput SLO and need a consistent, low-friction dashboard experience for SRE/CS and (later) a curated subset for customers. This PR extends the dedicated mixin dashboards with panels that (a) track throughput floors over time per customer/deployment, (b) show saturati...
text:
Context: We’re rolling out a formal Dedicated throughput SLO and need a consistent, low-friction dashboard experience for SRE/CS and (later) a curated subset for customers. This PR extends the dedicated mixin dashboards with panels that (a) track throughput floors over time per customer/deployment, (b) show saturation signals that correlate with SLO misses, and (c) provide drilldowns into the exact deployment/model/region slice.

What’s included: 1) “Throughput floor vs actual” panels: tokens/sec (per deployment_id) overlaid with computed floor (tier + shape) and SLO evaluator compliance; includes 5m/30m rollups with clear “below floor” highlighting. 2) “Saturation signals” row: admission-control shed rate, queue depth, in-flight requests, batcher wait time, KV cache hit ratio (when available), and GPU utilization proxy (SM occupancy / active time from node exporter where present). 3)...

title: Add Private backup/restore dashboards + alert rules (backup success, duration, restore drill outcomes)
preview: Context: As part of the Private Day-2 backup/restore toolkit, we need an opinionated observability pack that SRE/Solutions can deploy into customer VPC/on-prem environments to answer two questions quickly: (1) are backups happening and completing successfully on schedule, and (2) are restore drills passing and meeti...
text: Context: As part of the Private Day-2 backup/restore toolkit, we need an opinionated observability pack that SRE/Solutions can deploy into customer VPC/on-prem environments to answer two questions quickly: (1) are backups happening and completing successfully on schedule, and (2) are restore drills passing and meeting RTO targets in staging. This PR adds a set of dashboards and alert rules designed to work across hosted VPC installs and constrained on-prem/air-gapped setups (where available metrics are limited and log shipping may be disabled). Scope: (A) dashboards for backup job success rate, last_success_ts, duration, bytes, and error categories; (B) dashboards for restore drill outcomes (pass/fail, duration, validation phase breakdown) and drill cadence; (C) alert rules with conservative defaults plus documented tuning for noisy environments (slow object storage, intermittent KMS ...
links: Linear: ENG-9826 (metrics + alerts), ENG-9827 (restore drill automation)
